toolCollisionAnalysis.method: static README tool-table scrape (no code execution, no live tools/list)
toolCollisionAnalysis.note: Tool names were not statically recoverable from the recommended-install-snippet collection performed in Task 1 (README tool tables require dedicated per-server table parsing beyond the mcpServers-snippet scrape this pipeline does). Every one of the 100 servers is counted as skipped rather than fabricating a collision figure — this mirrors the scanner's own honesty-first framing (docs/mcp-security.md §5): tool-shadowing here is the static server-NAME collision proxy only (already reflected in tool-shadowing findings above), never verified tool-level shadowing.

disclosureCandidates.message: Server "s015__firecrawl-mcp" env var "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY" has a sensitive-sounding name but an inlined literal value (YOUR…[redacted]) — use `${env:FIRECRAWL_API_KEY}` or `${input:FIRECRAWL_API_KEY}` interpolation instead.
Server "s017___ui5_mcp-server"'s package "@ui5/mcp-server" reuses the exact name of a known scoped package under the unrecognized scope "@ui5" — possible scope-confusion squat.
